notes: Spec asserts the CORRECT behavior (not weakened to the bug): a gemini-cli foreground subagent (forced via @generalist) writes its own transcript under ~/.gemini/tmp/<project>/chats/<parentId>/session-<childId>.jsonl while the parent is alive; the daemon links it (parent_linked) and on a SIGKILL of the parent's process group the restart sweep reaps both parent and child without a spurious working transition for the orphaned child (issue #321). KNOWN_FAILING today: the daemon has no deriveParentSession rule for the gemini chats/<parentId>/ layout, so the child surfaces UNLINKED and the parent_linked phase fails (daemon=bug). The 1-child shape is sufficient: the no-working-transition invariant is identical for any N>=1.

text: Parent gemini session appears in ready
The @generalist prompt drives the parent ready -> working
Detector binds child.session_id -> parent.session_id (KNOWN_FAILING: no gemini parent-link rule today -> child surfaces unlinked)
SIGKILL on the parent's process group: pid_manager emits process_exited for the parent session

invariants: no state_transition to working for child after parent_killed
no state_transition to waiting for child after parent_killed
text: Restart sweep reaps the orphaned child without a spurious working transition (issue #321 invariant)
